title: Teaching Distribution Pipeline -- framework improvements propagated to all spokes
what: Teachings are markdown files (*.teaching) that encode framework improvements, new skills, and behavioral updates. The hub authors a teaching and places it in each spoke's WAI-Spoke/seed/ingest/. At session start, the wakeup protocol reads the ingest directory, adopts each teaching (installs skill files, applies config changes, updates fw_ver fingerprint), and archives to processed/.
why: Without teachings, every framework improvement requires manual editing of each spoke. Teachings are the automated distribution mechanism - author once in the hub, apply everywhere.

use_cases.title: Distributing a new skill file
use_cases.trigger: Hub authors skill-wai-savepoint-v1.md.teaching with the skill file embedded
use_cases.outcome: All spokes adopt at next session start - wai-savepoint.md appears in .claude/commands/

use_cases.title: Updating a behavioral rule via Doctor
use_cases.trigger: Hub authors a teaching that modifies CLAUDE.md anti-patterns
use_cases.outcome: Doctor rule pack executes on all spokes - CLAUDE.md updated consistently

patterns.name: safe_to_auto_adopt=true default
patterns.description: Default all teachings to safe_to_auto_adopt=true. Set false only for genuinely breaking changes.

patterns.name: Teaching weight contributes to fw_ver
patterns.description: Each teaching has weight (1/5/10/25). Absorbed teachings accumulate into spoke fw_ver fingerprint string.

steps_summary: 1. Hub authors teaching file with frontmatter: id, weight, safe_to_auto_adopt, target_files
2. Hub distributes to spoke ingest directories via outgoing/incoming transport
3. At spoke session start: wakeup scans seed/ingest/ for *.teaching files
4. For each: if safe_to_auto_adopt, apply immediately; else queue for manual review
5. Apply: install skill files, update configs, run Doctor rule packs as specified
6. Update fw_ver: append fingerprint = first 3 chars of md5('{id}:{weight}')
constraints: Teaching adoption (processed/ marker) does NOT guarantee skill file presence in .claude/commands/
fw_ver fingerprints are append-only and alpha-sorted for hash reproducibility

triggers: Hub publishes a teaching for distribution
not_when: Direct filesystem edits to spoke - always use teaching pipeline for cross-fleet changes

state_changes: Teaching moved from ingest/ to processed/

constraints: Never bypass teaching pipeline for cross-fleet behavioral changes

tests.coverage_area: teaching adoption lifecycle and fw_ver update

body: The teaching pipeline is the automated distribution mechanism for framework improvements across all spokes. A teaching is a markdown file with YAML frontmatter (id, title, type, version, safe_to_auto_adopt, created_at, weight) followed by a detailed description of what changed and how to apply it. The hub authors the teaching and places it in each spoke's WAI-Spoke/seed/ingest/ directory via the hub-spoke transport (outgoing/incoming). At session start, the wakeup protocol scans seed/ingest/ for *.teaching files and processes each one. If safe_to_auto_adopt=true the teaching is applied immediately; if false it is queued for manual review and displayed in the briefing as a pending item. Applying a teaching installs skill files (writes to .claude/commands/ or templates/commands/), updates configs, and runs Doctor rule packs as the teaching body specifies. After application, the teaching is archived to seed/ingest/processed/ and the spoke's fw_ver fingerprint string is updated. The fingerprint for a teaching is the first 3 characters of md5('{id}:{weight}'). Teaching weight values are: 1 (patch/minor fix), 5 (behavior update or protocol tweak), 10 (schema or protocol change), 25 (architectural addition or new advisor). Fingerprints are accumulated in chronological adoption order — the full fw_ver string is: {series}.{YYYYMMDD}-{fp1}.{fp2}.{fp3}. Two spokes with identical absorbed teachings produce identical fw_ver regardless of adoption order because the hash input sorts fingerprints alpha. A series increments when accumulated weight reaches 100 or when the hub emits a series-close teaching. Adoption (moving to processed/) does not guarantee skill file presence in .claude/commands/ — verification requires checking the filesystem directly. Every framework behavioral change must go through the teaching pipeline; direct spoke filesystem edits bypass the fleet and create invisible divergence.
done_when: A *.teaching file placed in WAI-Spoke/seed/ingest/ with safe_to_auto_adopt=true is applied and archived to seed/ingest/processed/ without user intervention during the next wakeup
After teaching application, WAI-State.json fw_ver contains the new teaching's fingerprint (first 3 chars of md5('{id}:{weight}')) appended to the previous fingerprint string
A teaching with safe_to_auto_adopt=false appears in the wakeup briefing under Pending Teachings with count and a prompt for manual action
Two spokes that have absorbed the same set of teachings in different orders produce identical fw_ver hash values
A teaching with weight=25 contributes 25 points toward the series accumulator, and the series increments when total accumulated weight reaches 100
